observations.managementAction:
The implementation of the Construction of Multi-purpose Building at Carig Sur, Tuguegarao City project is still on-going as of December 31, 2022. 

The Opening Tuguegarao By-pass Road-Bartolome Street, Caggay project, on the other hand, did not push through due to issues encountered on the acquisition of road right-of-way. The funds allocated thereto shall be reprogrammed to other socio-economic and development projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the City DRRM Officer and other concerned implementing offices to fully utilize the fund and implement all budgeted projects identified in its Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Investment Plan.

Moreover, we recommended that the CDRRMO re-evaluate the previously planned PPAs, especially those under the Continuing Appropriations, and determine the need for re-programming in order to better address the current needs of the City for disaster risk reduction and management. | AAR CY 2021
observations.managementAction:
The unexpended balances of the LDRRMF for CY 2021, resulting from the implementation of activities which did not require additional funding since sufficient supplies and materials exists and from the absence of serious calamities for which some programs and projects have been appropriated, have already been reprogrammed to other disaster risk reduction and management activities in CY 2022.

Moreover, procurement projects which were on-going as of December 31, 2021 have already been completed in CY 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management set its procurement for the projects programmed and funded under the 20% Development Fund at the earliest possible time and to fast track the implementation of all its programmed projects included in the Annual Investment Plan of the City in order for its constituents to enjoy the benefits to be derived therefrom. | AAR CY 2020
observations.managementAction: The implementation of the identified infrastructure projects was completed in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit the complete list of names of individuals accommodated in the hotels, indicating the period of their quarantine and the corresponding hotel room number, if practicable, to verify the accuracy of the cost of hotel accommodations charged to the City Government. | AAR CY 2020
observations.managementAction: The required list of frontline workers accommodated in some of the hotels were already submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Accountant strictly monitor the settlement of unliquidated cash advances and discontinue granting additional cash advances unless all outstanding cash advances have been liquidated. Subsequently, officials and employees with outstanding cash advances who get separated from the service should be required to execute an Affidavit of Undertaking that their outstanding cash advances will be paid upon receipt of their terminal leave pay. Failure of officers and employees who are still in service to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period shall constitute a valid cause to withhold their salary. 

We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive adopt strict measures to regulate the granting of cash advances in accordance with applicable provisions of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and adhere to Item 6.0 COA Circular 2012-004. Any unused or unspent cash advances should be returned or refunded in accordance with COA Memorandum No. 2017-010 dated May 15, 2017.
observations.managementAction: The unliquidated cash advances are deducted from the claims of the officials/employees concerned, such as their reimbursements or terminal leave benefits for those already separated from service. However, large amounts of cash advances issued in prior years remained unliquidated.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Observation No. 09 of Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accounting Office initiate the request for write-off of dormant items in the Other Receivables account in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.  Moreover, it is recommended that the City Accounting Office conduct a thorough analysis on the said account to determine the identity of the debtors.
observations.managementAction: Initial steps have been undertaken to cause the write-off of the dormant Other Receivables account.  However, no official request for write-off were submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Observation No. 06 of Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of all the PPE of the LGU and thereafter, prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), a copy of which should be submitted to the Office of the Auditor. The RPCPPE shall be reconciled with the PPE records or ledgers maintained at the Accounting Office and any discrepancy/ies should be adjusted.
observations.managementAction: Physical count was conducted for motor vehicles and heavy equipment only and its report was reconciled with the accounting records. However, the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment for other PPE accounts was not yet submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Observation No. 03 of Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the concerned departments to strictly implement COA Circular No. 2015-005 dated November 23, 2015 to fairly present the book value of the Road Networks account in the Financial Statements of the City Government.
observations.managementAction: Separate subsidiary ledgers containing complete description of the roads and its components were not maintained. Moreover, the Report on Local Road Network, using the prescribed format, was not submitted.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Observation No. 08 of Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the City General Services Office and City Accounting Office and a representative of the City Schools Division Office/DepEd, to conduct a physical inventory of the aforementioned assets and prepare an Inventory Report containing all information about the existing and serviceable assets for its eventual transfer to the City Schools Division Office.  

Further, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive coordinate with the Head of the recipient Agency for proper turn-over of the assets. Thereafter, the City Accounting Office shall draw the necessary Journal Entry Voucher to derecognize the PPE to be transferred and furnish a copy of the JEV to the Schools Division Office/DepEd in order for the latter to record the same in their books of accounts. Other necessary documents pertaining to the assets should also be turned-over to them.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Observation No. 05 of Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Accountant to review and analyze the Accounts Payable which had been outstanding for two (2) years or more.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management set its procurement for the projects programmed and funded under the 20% Development Fund at the earliest possible time and to fast track the implementation of all its programmed projects included in the Annual Investment Plan of the City in order for its constituents to enjoy the benefits to be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction:
The implementation of the identified infrastructure projects was completed in CY 2021.

It should be noted however, that delays were incurred in the implementation of projects, programs and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund for CY 2021.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Observation No. 23 of the Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management submit the complete list of names of individuals accommodated in the hotels, indicating the period of their quarantine and the corresponding hotel room number, if practicable, to verify the accuracy of the cost of hotel accommodations charged to the City Government.

With regards to the initial computation of overcharging, the management of Sunflower Drive Inn, Balai Carmela and Zurcs Hotel are likewise required to explain their side. Presentation of proof to the contrary is encouraged.  If determined that there is indeed overcharging, the concerned hotels/inns shall refund to the City the amount of overcharged cost of hotel accommodation.
observations.managementAction:
The required list of frontline workers accommodated in some of the hotels were not yet submitted.




The overpayment to Sunflower Drive Inn amounting to P133,000.00 was already refunded.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Observation No. 10 of the Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Accounting Office regularly monitor the preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statements by the personnel in-charge and determine causes of delay so that this can be addressed immediately, thus ensuring the timely preparation and submission of the said reports.
The personnel in-charge should immediately request at the end of the month the bank statements, paid checks, debit and credit memos from the banks to ensure availability of the documents needed in the preparation of the BRS. This will ensure its timely submission to the Audit Team and other concerned offices in strict compliance with Item 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended that Management:
exert efforts to locate all necessary documents of sites which were donated and acquired through purchase and pursue the titling in the name of the City Government of Tuguegarao; and
assign one personnel to take charge in determining the value of the 17 lots with a total area of 157,108 square meters already in the name of the City that are not yet recorded and record the same in the books of accounts of the City.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create a task force committee that can responsibly and knowledgeably do the conduct of physical count of PPE of the LGU.   If possible, assign three to five personnel from each division/office as members of the committee in order to facilitate the counting and determination of whereabouts of items located in their office.
The Property Officer in coordination with the committee, shall be responsible in the over-all preparation and submission of the required Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment as of December 31, 2021.

We recommended that the City Accountant assign one personnel to prepare schedules/list of the infrastructure assets and to look into the source documents as basis in determining the details, description and useful life thereof which is needed in the computation of the depreciation. The accomplishment of the person assigned should be closely monitor in order to address any problems encountered in the delay in the computation and/or completion of the schedule of the depreciation.   It is stressed that the updated recording of the corresponding depreciation   will ensure fair presentation of the various infrastructure assets accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Accounting Office to prepare a lists/schedule of the PPE being transferred  based on the existing subsidiary ledgers of the PPE accounts.
Thereafter, create an inventory team composed of City General Services Office and City Accounting Office, if possible with a representative of the Schools Division Office/DepEd to conduct physical count thereafter to determine their existence as basis for the turn-over of the existing and serviceable PPE.

We recommended that the City Accounting Office assign one personnel to take charge in the preparation of the required documents for the request for write-off of dormant accounts in the total amount of P4,838,241.24 in accordance with applicable provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 and immediately submit the same to COA for evaluation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive give an ultimatum to the City Accountant and other concerned employees responsible in  the maintenance and recording of transactions in the books of  accounts  to conduct a thorough analysis to determine the root cause of the negative balances by assigning  specific personnel  responsible in the conduct of  reconciliation of specific payable accounts with negative balances  in order to  fix responsibility and for proper monitoring of accomplishments.
We further recommended that the Accounting staff responsible in the recording of transactions to ensure proper accounting entry in the recording of payables in the books of the City and should be thoroughly reviewed by the City Accountant or authorized personnel to ensure that correct account is used in accordance with the Government Accounting Manual for Local Government Units.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create a team to work on the details/composition of the roads, highways and bridges previously recorded in the Registry of Public Infrastructure (RPI) which were already transferred to the Road Networks account in lump sum amount of P474,337,255.28 as of December 31, 2021, to ensure that the booked amount reflects the correct value of the road networks, thus rendering the account reliable.

We recommended that the City Accountant strictly monitor the settlement of unliquidated cash advances. Subsequently, officials and employees with outstanding cash advances who get separated from the service should be required to execute an Affidavit of Undertaking that their outstanding cash advances will be paid upon receipt of their terminal leave pay. And for those officers and employees still in the service, their failure to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period shall constitute a valid cause to withhold their salary.
We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive adopt strict measures to regulate the granting of cash advances in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and adhere to Item 6.0 of COA Circular 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012. Any unused or unspent cash advances should be returned or refunded.

We recommended that the Management secure from the Tuguegarao City Peoples General Hospital the certified list of frontline workers who were accommodated in Villa Blanca Hotel to ensure validity and propriety on the payment of hotel accommodation in the amount of P3,194,531.25, and submit the same to COA for audit purposes.
Likewise, we requested the immediate submission of the required list of names and the statement of account to support the claim of D128 Lodge in the amount of P278,400.00.  Otherwise, these transactions shall be suspended in audit.

We recommended that the Infrastructure Projects Unit submit immediately the plans/drawings for the four projects on COVID-19 PPAs (Repair and Maintenance of COVID facilities) with a total cost of P4,269,646.10 to ensure propriety and validity in the conduct of the procurement and likewise needed in the conduct of technical inspection thereof.

We recommended that Management ensure that all the required information on lists of recipients or distribution lists for projects of the City that involve the distribution of goods purchased are completely filled-out and properly accomplished in order to prevent confusion as to the actual number of goods distributed and the remaining balance of goods to be recognized as an asset, thereby ensuring proper accounting thereof.

We recommended that the City Accounting Office ensure that all payments to be made for the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects through alternative modes are supported with the documentary requirements prescribed under applicable provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.
Further, we recommended that the lacking documents be submitted immediately to facilitate the review of the aforementioned transactions.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee ensure that all required information pertaining to the terms of delivery in the Purchase Order be completely filled-out in order for the City General Services Office to properly monitor the implementation of the projects and to prevent the incurrence of delays in its completion.

We recommended that the City Accounting Office submit the lacking documentary requirements to support various disbursements on the payment of advertising expenses   amounting to P1,217,500.00 to ensure propriety and validity of the transactions in compliance with Items G, L(1), L(2) and Item II of Appendix A of Annex H of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184. Otherwise, these will be suspended in audit.

We recommended that the Management ensure that contract costs for the City’s various procurement projects do not exceed their Approved Budget for the Contract in faithful adherence to Section 7.1 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 in order to prevent the incurrence of excessive expenditures as defined under COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee carefully perform detailed evaluation before post-qualification of eligible bids to ensure that computational errors and omissions are considered and to correctly identify the lowest calculated and responsive bid, in faithful adherence to Sections 32.2.1(b) and 32.2.4 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184, and to avoid disallowance of transactions.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee carefully perform detailed evaluation during post-qualification of eligible bids to ensure that computational errors and omissions are considered before the execution of the contract between the City and the winning bidder and prevent the incurrence of excessive expenditures as defined under COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.
Likewise, refund of the excess of the re-calculated bid price over the submitted financial bid proposal of the contractor amounting to P45,438.79 is enjoined.

We recommended that Management ensure that retention monies to be withheld from payments to suppliers are in the amount prescribed under Section 62.1 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 in order to provide adequate protection in case of defects in the goods purchased by the City.

We recommended that variation orders issued by Management be within the threshold prescribed under Section 1.1 of Annex E of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 and that an explanation or justification be submitted for the excess of the amount of variation order over the prescribed percentage of the contract price.

We recommended that the Head of the Agency, who is primarily responsible for all government funds and property, ensure the settlement of disallowances in the amount of P1,566,656.04 within the prescribed period.
Further, the City Accountant should draw the necessary journal entries to adjust the unrecorded settlements and erroneous recording of settlements in the amount of P158,000.00 and P4,800.00, respectively to establish the correct balance of the Receivables-Disallowances/Charges account.
Likewise, we requested the submission of the disbursement vouchers showing the deduction of disallowances from the terminal leave benefits and from payroll evidencing the settlements amounting to P43,727.22.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to expedite the procurement process for the Construction of Multi-purpose Building at Carig Sur, Tuguegarao City and Road Opening Tuguegarao By-pass Road-Bartolome Street, Caggay projects with a total cost of P31,579,490.00 to ensure that public funds are utilized at an optimal level and the benefits that could be derived therefrom are immediately delivered to the intended constituents/beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the City DRRM Officer and other concerned implementing offices to fully utilize the fund and implement all budgeted projects identified in its Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Investment Plan.
Moreover, we recommended that the CDRRMO re-evaluate the previously planned PPAs, especially those under the Continuing Appropriations, and determine the need for re-programming in order to better address the current needs of the City for disaster risk reduction and management.

We recommended that the Management limit the amount of cash advances to be granted to the Disbursing Officers within the maximum accountability stated in the bond policy in compliance to Item 5.1 of the Treasury Circular No. 02-2019.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Accountant and other concerned employees and heads of other offices, where the documents passed through prior to the recording in the City Accounting Office, to conduct a thorough analysis to determine the root cause of this irregularity.  From this, the appropriate course of action could be done to correct the negative balances and the various reconciling items noted. 

We further recommended that the Accounting staff responsible in the recording of transactions determine whether the payable account was credited to recognize the liability of the City prior to the debiting of the appropriate subsidiary payable account.  Management should also ensure that the amount paid to the payees is equal to the amount of payable initially set up for the particular transaction.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office conducted an analysis in a portion of the payable accounts under the General Fund with debit balances. The JEV of the corresponding correcting entries drawn to remove the abnormal balances was not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit to the Office of the Auditor the perfected contract agreements for the 18 infrastructure projects to avoid imposition of the penalty clause embodied in Item 4 of COA Circular 2009-001 and suspension in audit of payments made.
observations.managementAction: The contracts were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity require the BAC, through its Secretariat, to post the Invitation to Bid, the Notice of Award and the Notice to Proceed in the agency website for every procurements conducted through competitive bidding to be compliant with applicable provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and for wider dissemination of bid opportunities for contracting parties, who are eligible and qualified to participate in competitive bidding and in informing the public of the results of the procurement processes.
observations.managementAction: The City’s website was restored.  The Invitation to Bid, Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed for succeeding projects of the City were appropriately posted in the website.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity require the submission of the Construction Safety and Health Program approved by the Department of Labor and Employment before the perfection or signing of the contract and ensure that the CSHP is being implemented on project site.
observations.managementAction: The CSHP approved by the DOLE is attached in each contract for the infrastructure projects of the City procured in CY 2021 through public bidding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management refrain from using the brand name as description/specification of goods to be procured in the purchase request and canvass forms, instead, they shall use relevant characteristics and/or performance requirements pursuant to Section 18 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Management no longer uses the brand name as description for goods to be procured.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the Contractor, Square Meter Trading and Construction, to submit a new Performance Bond to replace the expired bond, to guarantee the contractor’s faithful performance of its obligations under the contract.
observations.managementAction: The Management required the contractor to secure a new performance bond for the second year of the project implementation and the same was submitted to the Audit Team for verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Engineering Office to prepare and submit monthly the Statement of Time Elapsed and Accomplished, especially for projects with longer project durations, in order to monitor monthly the status of implementation of the projects.
observations.managementAction:
The City Engineering Office already submitted the Statement of Time Elapsed and Accomplished for the subject project.

Likewise, the said Office also prepares the aforementioned document for its various infrastructure projects and attaches them to their corresponding disbursement vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management exercise prudence in the examination of the documents submitted, particularly the performance security bonds, to ensure that these are supported with official receipts and are effective and enforceable and to ensure indemnity in cases of default in the implementation of projects.
observations.managementAction: For infrastructure projects implemented in CY 2021, the original official receipts of the performance bond and CARI are attached to the contracts submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management ensure that all contracts executed are processed accordingly and supported with complete documentary requirements prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012 – 001. Further, we recommended that the lacking documents be submitted immediately, and the deficiencies noted are corrected to ensure validity and regularity of the contracts executed.
observations.managementAction: The Management submitted the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that all Small Value Procurement processes are faithfully complied with and that all contract agreements under this alternative mode of procurement are supported with complete documentary requirements prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012 – 001.
observations.managementAction: The Infrastructure Projects unit of the City, designated to undertake the procurement of goods and services thru negotiated procurement (Small Value Procurement), submits contracts with complete documentary requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strictly observe the aforementioned provisions of GPPB Circular 01-2020 in all of its emergency procurement under the Bayanihan to Heal as One Act.  Moreover, we required the submission of the lacking documentary requirements to the Audit Team for verification.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee complied with the posting requirement for the aforementioned procurement projects under Bayanihan to Heal as One Act.  The required documentary requirements were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Accountant ensure that the appropriate withholding tax rates are imposed payments for goods received and services rendered to the City in compliance with Section 116 of the National Internal Revenue Code and relevant provisions of Revenue Regulation No. 11-2018 in order to avoid the over or under withholding of tax.  

Moreover, we required that the amount of under withheld taxes totaling P8,750.00 from payments to Balai Carmela and Villa Blanca Hotel be collected therefrom and remitted to the Bureau of Internal Revenue.  In their failure to do so, the person responsible in computing withholding taxes shall be held accountable thereto.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office deducted the under withheld taxes from the disbursement vouchers of .  The GSO is still coordinating with the management of Balai Carmela for the refund of amount of under withheld tax.Villa Blanca Hotel

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the City DRRM Officer and other concerned implementing offices to fully utilize the fund and implement all budgeted projects identified in its Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Investment Plan.  Moreover, we recommended that the CDRRMO act on the immediate preparation and approval of the investment plan for the NDRRM Fund so that the fund can be utilized to address the current needs of the City for disaster risk reduction and management.
observations.managementAction:
The CDRRM Council approved the inclusion of the P23 Million received from the NDRRMC and the reprogramming of other unimplemented PPAs in the revised LDRRMFIP through CDRRMC Resolution Nos. 1 and 2, series of 2021, respectively. 
The Disaster and Rescue, Search and Emergency Transport Equipment project is already implemented while the Climate Change Mitigation and Resiliency program and the Community-Based Early Warning System program are under implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management submit the GAD Accomplishment Report in order for the Team to evaluate the implementation of the GAD programs and projects and the judicious utilization of the fund.
observations.managementAction:
The GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2020 was submitted.

The GAD Accomplishment Report for CY 2021 was submitted on February 28, 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management utilize the unused portion of the third floor of the Tuguegarao City Commercial Center for use of the barangays as their barangay halls.  This will enable the City to provide for the facility needs of the barangays, while protecting the interest of the City Government.
observations.managementAction: Of the five (5) mentioned barangays, only Barangay Centro 07 occupies a space under lease in the third floor. The Management also granted the use of some unused stalls at the third floor to the Philippine National Police - SOCO for their office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that remittances to Pag-Ibig should be in accordance with the provisions of HDMF Circular No. 275 to avoid the imposition of penalties.  Moreover, we recommended that the City investigate the cause of imposition of the penalty and require the responsible City official or employee to resolve the matter and to refund the penalty amounting to P7,558.71.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office remits the Pag-ibig contributions in accordance with the provisions of HDMF Circular.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the General Services Office to observe strictly the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and Item A.2 of COA Circular No. 96-010 by submitting a copy of all contracts and Purchase Orders within five (5) working days from its execution and notify the Auditor of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Office submitted the copies of the contracts/purchase orders to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accounting Office initiate the request for write-off for dormant items in the Other Receivables Account in accordance with the procedures set-out in COA Circular No. 2016-005.  Moreover, it is recommended that the City Accounting Office conduct a thorough examination on the said account to determine the identity of the debtors that should have been identified instead of the reconciling items appearing in the schedule for the account.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of all the PPE of the LGU and thereafter, prepare an inventory report, i.e. the RPCPPE, a copy of which be submitted to the Office of the Auditor. The inventory report shall be reconciled with the PPE records or ledgers maintained at the Accounting Office and any discrepancy/ies should be adjusted.
observations.managementAction: The Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment was not yet submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.managementAction: No action was taken by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the withholding agent prudently monitor the withholding of tax in compliance with Section 80(A) of National Internal Revenue Code.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management,

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Government endeavor to look for a private developer partner to finish the construction of the Tuguegarao City Commercial Center and to lease out the commercial space to a qualified lessee to protect the interest of the City Government.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Management.  On a positive note, the Management agreed to allow the use of the third floor of the Tuguegarao City Commercial Center for the barangay halls of Barangays Centro 2, 4, 5, 7 and 8.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Accounting Office:
Regularly and promptly prepare monthly BRS for all bank accounts of the City;
For the unrecorded bank credit memos amounting to P200,000.00, coordinate with the bank for the submission of credit advices or other substitute documents that would give information on the nature of the transactions that would support their eventual recording in the books. Same with the reconciling item amounting to P269,471.81 in which the City Accounting Office is unsure whether those are credit memos or unrecorded deposits because they are not familiar with some of the codes used by the banks.
Likewise, communicate with the concerned banks to correct or adjust the errors in their records; and
For the untransmitted disbursement vouchers amounting to P27,164.64, together with the City Treasurer’s Office, locate the approved disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents that were not yet transmitted to the City Accounting Office.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive adopt strict measures to regulate the granting of cash advances in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and Item 6.0 COA Circular 2012-004. Any unused or unspent cash advances should be returned or refunded.
We further recommended that the City Accountant strictly monitor the settlement of unliquidated cash advances and discontinue granting additional cash advances unless all outstanding cash advances have been liquidated. Subsequently, officials and employees with outstanding cash advances who get separated from the service should be required to execute an Affidavit of Undertaking that their outstanding cash advances will be paid upon receipt of their terminal leave pay. And for those officers and employees still in the service, their failure to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period shall constitute a valid cause to withhold their salary.

We recommended that the Head of the Agency, who is primarily responsible for all government funds and property, ensure that the settlement of disallowances be made within the prescribed period.
Further, the City Accountant should record all disallowances which have become  final and executory amounting to P1,137,402.00 upon receipt of the Notices of Finality of Decision issued by the Auditor. She should also make the necessary adjustments for noted errors in their records to establish the correct balance of the affected accounts Receivables-Disallowances and Charges and Government Equity.
The submission of the disbursement vouchers showing the deduction of disallowances from the terminal leave benefits and the official receipts evidencing the settlements amounting to P231,141.14 was requested.

We recommended that the City Accounting Office conduct a thorough reconciliation of the Other Receivables account to correct the negative balances and the other reconciling items noted.  Moreover, for dormant items in the account which were not properly supported with valid documents, it is recommended that the City Accounting Office initiate the request for write-off in accordance with the procedures set-out in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the concerned departments to strictly follow and implement the provisions contained in the above mentioned Circular to fairly present the book value of the Road Networks account in the Financial Statements of the City Government.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Accounting Office to transfer to the agencies concerned the book value of all properties being used and operated by agencies other than the Local Government Unit of Tuguegarao City to conform with IPSAS 17 – Property, Plant and Equipment. The City Accounting Office should draw the necessary Journal Entry Voucher to derecognize the PPE transferred to other agencies and furnish a copy of the JEV, together with the Inventory Listing, to the recipient agencies in order for those agencies to record the same in their books of accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Inventory Committee to  conduct physical inventory of all the PPE of the LGU and thereafter, prepare an inventory report, i.e. the RPCPPE, a copy of which be submitted to the Office of the Auditor. The inventory report shall be reconciled with the PPE records or ledgers maintained at the Accounting Office and any discrepancy/ies should be adjusted.

We recommended that the Management require the TLDO to revisit its Work and Financial Plan and identify the activities that still need to be implemented, and facilitate the implementation of programs with a low percentage of utilization.  This is to ensure that all the targeted beneficiaries enjoy the benefits from the full implementation of the programs.

We recommended that Management fast track the implementation of all its programmed projects included in the Annual Investment Plan of the City in order for its constituents, especially the students/pupils to enjoy the benefits to be derived therefrom.

We recommended that the BAC Secretariat post the Notice of Award, Contract and Notice to Proceed for projects procured through Small Value Procurement in a manner provided in the relevant provisions of RA 9184.  It is also recommended that the Performance Security and Warranty Security be required from the contractor pursuant to Section 54.5 of the same rules.

We recommended that the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) be prepared and submitted as part of the documents to support the contract agreement in compliance with Section 17.1 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and to facilitate review of contract agreements.

We required an explanation or justification to be submitted as to why the approved budget of contract for the project as indicated in the Invitation to Bid, APP and PPMP is higher than its estimated cost shown in the Program of Work.

We recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity require the BAC, through its Secretariat, to advertise and/or post every Invitation to Bid, Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed within the prescribed period and using the required media in compliance with the relevant provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 and to ensure transparency in the procurement process of the City.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee exercise prudence in the examination of bidding documents submitted by prospective bidders to ensure that they are eligible to participate in the bidding and be awarded of the contract in faithful adherence to pertinent provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.

We recommended that Management require the contractor, Cavat Capitol Merchandising and Construction, to submit the Contractor’s Affidavit of Payment of Equipment Rentals/Lease to ensure that the City Government is free from any legal claims of third party/ies that may arise against the contractor.

We recommended that Management strictly follow the provision of the above Circular to promote good governance, transparency and accountability and to accord the public of right and truthful information.

We recommended that the LCE direct the General Services Office to observe strictly the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and Item A.2 of COA Circular No. 96-010 by submitting a copy of all contracts and Purchase Orders within five (5) working days from its execution and notify the Auditor of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries within the prescribed period.
We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that all payments are supported with complete documentary requirements prescribed by Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012 – 001.

We recommended that Management grant honoraria to the BAC Members, BAC-TWG and BAC Secretariat within the  25% limitation set forth under Section 15 of the Revised IRR and Item 5.4 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A.   Moreover, in cases of deficiency of collections from procurement activities and availability of agency savings, the amount of honoraria  and overtime pay, if any, shall be adjusted proportionately for all those entitled thereto, pursuant to Item 3.5 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3.
Finally, it is recommended that the personnel of the BAC Secretariat who are assigned in the procuring unit of the CGSO shall, henceforth, not be granted with honoraria.
The Audit Team stressed that the excess honoraria totaling to P287,953.50 should be refunded by the affected BAC Members, TWG and Secretariat.

We recommended that the City Accounting Office compute the amount of excess collections for the sale of bid documents recorded in the Other Payables account maintained in the Trust Fund and cause its transfer to the General Fund, pursuant to the provisions of the aforementioned Budget Circular.  Moreover, when the excessive disbursements amounting to P216,500.00 is returned, the amount of P216,910.24 should be transferred to the General Fund.

We recommended that the City DRRM Officer and other concerned implementing offices: (a) fully utilize the fund and implement all budgeted projects, programs and activities;(b) submit monthly to the Audit Team the Report on the Sources and Utilization of the Funds, certified by the City Accountant, for proper and timely evaluation; and (c) include in the LDRRMFIP the activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years, which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund.

We recommended that the City Treasurer and City Accountant coordinate with the Head of the CDRRMO on the availability of donated funds and utilize the amount for programs/projects that serve the specific intent and purpose of the donation, to optimize utilization thereof and timely bring the desired benefits to the intended beneficiaries.
Likewise, we recommended that the concerned City Officials utilize the amount received from the City Government of Makati for programs/projects to address disaster preparedness on the impact of likely, imminent or current hazard events or conditions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the head GSO to mark “For Official Use Only” and to acquire government plates for all  motor vehicles of the LGU in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 to ensure the effective conservation of energy and proper utilization of government motor transportation.

We recommended that Management exert its best effort in searching for a Private Sector Proponent that will finish the construction and will conduct maintenance of the Tuguegarao City Commercial Center because chances of finding one is diminishing as time passes by due to tight competition with large private malls operating and for other malls who will be operating in the future.  The completion of the third and fourth levels of the commercial center, leasing it out on commercial basis and conducting maintenance and beautification will not only protect the interest of the City, but will ultimately benefit its constituents in terms of better services.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that all concerned City Officials immediately undertake appropriate actions to facilitate proper execution of laws and policies governing the revision of appraisal and assessment of real properties for taxation purposes to increase the collection of local taxes.

We recommended that the City Government empower the barangays in its major roles and jurisdiction of waste collection of biodegradable and non-biodegradable wastes while the City Government will focus and perform its jurisdiction of waste collection of residual and special waste only.

We recommended Management to strictly implement the “No Segregation, No Collection Policy” in the collection of solid wastes and to provide  garbage trucks equipped with closed containers where collected wastes are contained during the collection period for the realization of the City Government’s vision and mission. To fully implement this policy, we suggest that scheduling of collection of every type of waste be observed and imposed. Every Mondays, Wednesdays and Fridays, biodegradable wastes are collected, Tuesdays and Thursdays, non-biodegradable wastes are collected, and on Saturdays, special or hazardous wastes. This will at least encourage wastes providers to observe proper segregation of wastes at source and likewise, the City Government to strictly observe and implement the segregated collection.
We recommended that the City Government use and operate the landfill cell to comply with the requirement of Republic Act No. 9003, otherwise known as Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.

We recommended that mandatory remittances be made in accordance with the provisions of HDMF Circular No. 275 to avoid the possible imposition of penalties. We further recommended that Management prudently monitor the deduction of loans and ensure faithful compliance with the pertinent provision of R.A. No. 8291.
We recommended that the withholding agent prudently monitor the withholding of tax in compliance with Section 80(A) of National Internal Revenue Code.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the employee in charge of the approval of loans to ensure that the net take home pay of each employee shall in no case be lower than ₱5,000.00 and also monitor the allowable deductions in the order of preference as required by Section 48 of the General Provisions of Republic Act No. (RA) 10964, the General Appropriations Act for Fiscal Year 2018.
We recommended that the LCE require the Heads of the implementing departments/offices to ensure that the GAD programs, projects and activities are implemented as planned to attain the GAD Objectives.
We recommended that the LCE establish a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation System as required under Items 3.4 and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

4. We recommended that the  City  Accountant  and  the  City  Treasurer
        reconcile regularly the balances appearing in  the  General  Ledgers
        and the Cashbooks in compliance with Section 6.3 of COA Circular No.
        97-002 to arrive at a correct balances  of  the  Cash  on  hand  and
        Advances for Payrolls accounts.

5. We recommended that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  advise  the  City
        Treasurer’s  Office  to  submit  or  transmit  daily  to  the   City
        Accounting Office the disbursement vouchers for  all  checks  issued
        during the day for the latter to record in the books. This will  not
        only  eliminate  the  occurrence  of   unstransmitted   disbursement
        vouchers from the City Treasurer’s Office but will also reflect  the
        realistic balance of Cash in Bank, LCCA at the end of the day.

7. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive adopt strict  measures
        to regulate the granting of cash advances  in  accordance  with  the
        provisions of COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and adhere
        to Item 6.0 COA  Circular  2012-004.  Any  unused  or  unspent  cash
        advances should be returned or refunded.


     8. We further recommended that the City Accountant strictly monitor the
        settlement of unliquidated cash advances  and  discontinue  granting
        additional cash advances unless all outstanding cash  advances  have
        been  liquidated.  Subsequently,  officials   and   employees   with
        outstanding cash advances who get separated from the service  should
        not   be   cleared   from   money/property   accountability   unless
        liquidations are made.

7. We recommended that the persons liable be required to  settle  their
        audit disallowances immediately, otherwise, on the basis of the  COA
        Order of Execution or COE, their salaries and  any  money  due  them
        shall be withheld by the City Treasurer for the settlement thereof.


     8. Further, the City Accountant should record all  disallowances  which
        have become final and  executory  amounting  to  P1,332,285.22.  She
        should also verify  and  make  the  necessary  adjustments  for  the
        variance of P9,793.41  to  establish  the  correct  balance  of  the
        affected  accounts   Receivables-Disallowances   and   Charges   and
        Government Equity.

5.     We recommended  that  the  City  Accountant  should  review  the
        Journal Entry Voucher to ensure that all transactions  are  properly
        and accurately recorded  in  the  books  of  accounts,  analyze  the
        reciprocal accounts and make necessary adjustments  to  reflect  the
        correct balances in order to present the actual financial  condition
        of  the  City  Government  as  of  December  31,   2017.    Further,
        explanation is  also  requested  for  the  non-adjustment  of  these
        accounts.

5.     We recommended  to  the  Local  Chief  Executive  to  create  or
        constitute an inventory committee to conduct physical  inventory  of
        all the PPE of the LGU and thereafter, prepare an inventory  report,
        i.e. the RPCPPE, a copy of which be submitted to the Office  of  the
        Auditor. The inventory report  shall  be  reconciled  with  the  PPE
        records or ledgers maintained  at  the  Accounting  Office  and  any
        discrepancy/ies should be adjusted.

8.     We recommended that  the  City  Accountant  draw  the  necessary
        journal entry voucher to reclassify and record the costs of the Mall
        of the  Valley  as  Construction  in  Progress-Investment  Property,
        Buildings  amounting  to  Ᵽ525,061,604.62  (Ᵽ504,709,243.58  in  the
        General Fund, Ᵽ20,352,361.04  in the Trust Fund), to record the cost
        of one (1) unit concrete mixer of Ᵽ1,148,000.00  to Construction and
        Heavy Equipment account, and to provide disclosures in the Notes  to
        Financial Statements of the present condition of  the  building  for
        fair presentation of the financial statements of the LGU.

5.     We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the  City
        Accounting Office in coordination  with  the  Veterinary  Office  to
        conduct an appraisal of the breeding stocks and recognize the result
        in the books of accounts to reflect the correct and realistic  value
        of the Biological Asset account of the LGU.

7. We reiterated our prior year’s  recommendation  that  all  concerned
        City  Officials  immediately  undertake   appropriate   actions   to
        facilitate proper execution  of  laws  and  policies  governing  the
        revision of appraisal and assessment of real properties for taxation
        purposes to increase the collection of local taxes.

6. We recommend that the Bids and  Awards  Committee  carefully  assess
        thoroughly the documents submitted by prospective bidders to  ensure
        that they pass all eligibility criteria.

8. We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee  carefully  review
        the amendments in the provisions of the Revised  Implementing  Rules
        and Regulations of R. A. 9184 to ensure that all of  its  provisions
        are followed in bidding for  various  procurement  projects  of  the
        City.  Further, the  Bids  and  Awards  Committee  should  carefully
        review the documents submitted by prospective bidders to ensure that
        they pass all the eligibility  and  post-qualification  criteria  to
        avoid the irregularities noted.

observations.managementAction: Demand letters were issued to concerned officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances as of August 31, 2017.
Management complied and submitted the lacking supporting documents to the COA Audit Team in compliance with and whenever AOMs are issued.

The City Accountant certified that the previous fund transfers to barangays are liquidated and accounted for in the books of the LGU. Financial assistance to barangays are now covered by a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA).

Management complied and explained in their answer that the process of importing brand new vehicles includes the registration thereof in the name of the importer. Hence, units sold and delivered to the City by the supplier bears the name of the importer.

There were partial settlement of disallowances which were deducted from claims of persons liable, Aside from the disallowances under appeal of P13,358,351.46, audit disallowances of P2,332,109.40 remaned unsettled as of December 31, 2017. We reiterated our recommendation.

observations.recommendation: NSWMC expedite the submission of the additional requirements contained in Resolution No. 786 series of 2016 to prevent further delay in the approval of the Local Solid Waste Management Plan of the City.
observations.managementAction: The Solid Waste Management (SWM) Plan reckoning from 2016-2025 was updated by CENRO and was submitted to the National Solid Waste Mgmt. Commission for their comment and approval after it was evaluated by the Coordinator of Region 02 and presented to the TWG of the NSWMC.

observations.recommendation: Considering that the request for debt relief has not yet been acted upon or granted by the ESF Secretariat/Department of Finance, we reiterate our recommendation for Management to appropriate funds for the payment of the loan since the same has already become due and demandable. Moreover, Management should follow-up persistently the status of their request for debt relief.
observations.managementAction: No follow-up action was taken by management on the status of the request for write-off from the ESF-DOF. The City had already allotted funds for debt servicing of the outstanding loan.

observations.recommendation: Management fast track the implementation of all the unimplemented programs and projects of the LGU and ensure that future projects to be included in the Annual Investment Plan of the City are implemented as scheduled.
observations.managementAction: The projects were already completed in 2017, but the project for road opening was not implemented due to road right of way issues.

observations.recommendation: Management ensure the timely utilization of special trust funds and donations for disaster risk reduction and Management activities in order to improve the City’s ability to prepare for and respond to threats brought about by various calamities and disasters.
observations.managementAction: The CDRRMC had reprogrammed the 2012 unexpended balances of the LDRRMF Special Trust Fund. Donations were already utilized for the purchase of GI sheets.

observations.recommendation: Management expedite the implementation of various programs, projects and activities for which the fund transfers from various government agencies were received to prevent further delay in the attainment of the intended benefits.
observations.managementAction: Intended program, projects and activities of the fund were already physically implemented, only that the suppliers/contractors were not yet paid.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to Management the following: A ramp or properly situated hand rail should be provided at the entrance of the ABHS so that clients and patients with physical difficulties need not struggle in entering the ABHS. The flooring inside the ABHS should be even so that individuals need not step up or down within the premises. Pending reconstruction, a signage should be placed in this area to give notice to the clients on the unleveled floor.
observations.managementAction: A ramp and a hand rail at the entrance of the ABHS was already provided.

observations.recommendation: The City Health Officer require the BHS administrators to submit the budget of their respective BHS and their need requirements for medicines and supplies during the preparation of the Annual Budget and the Project Procurement Management Plan to ensure that the supplies, medicines, equipment and other needs of the BHS are included therein.
observations.managementAction: During the preparation of annual budget, midwifes and nurses were enjoined in the identification of commodities and supplies needed by each BJHS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management ensure that all payments to be made for the procurement of goods be supported with the documentary requirements prescribed under Sections 9.1 and 9.1.3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001. Further, we recommended that the lacking documents be submitted immediately to facilitate the review of the subject-procurement activities.
observations.managementAction: It was noted that in CY 2016, various procurements of goods and infrastructure projects remain unsupported by relevant documentary requirements prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012 – 001.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the persons liable be required to settle immediately their audit disallowances, otherwise, on the basis of the COA Order of Execution or COE, their salaries and any money due them shall be withheld by the City Treasurer for the settlement thereof. 15.7. Further, the City Accountant should make the necessary adjustments for the variance of P23,282.27 to establish the correct balance of the affected accounts Receivables-Disallowances and Charges and Government Equity.
observations.managementAction:
Out of the audit disallowances issued in prior years totaling P10,543,747.67, only P638,546.33 or 6.06% were settled during the year, leaving an unsettled balance of P9,905,201.34 as of December 31, 2016.

An amount of P12,578.27 which pertains to erroneous recording of settlements/ disallowances made in prior periods remain unadjusted.

observations.recommendation: Considering that the request for debt relief has not yet been acted upon or granted by the ESF Secretariat/Department of Finance, we recommended for management to appropriate additional funds for the payment of the loan since the same has already become due and demandable. Moreover, management should follow-up persistently the status of their request for debt relief.
observations.managementAction: The City Government appropriated an initial amount of P5,000,000 in CY 2015. Additional amounts shall be appropriated through supplemental budget as soon as there is a notice furnished by the Department of Finance.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that management first appropriate funds for the payment of the LGU’s contractual obligations before granting honoraria to national government officials assigned in the City pursuant to DBM Compensation Policy Guidelines No. 98-1.
observations.managementAction: An amount of P3,953,860 was disbursed as honoraria to national government officials assigned in the City for CY 2016 despite the existence of outstanding contractual obligations.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the prior year’s recommendation that all real properties acquired by the LGU either through purchase or donation be titled in the name of the City Government.
observations.managementAction:
Ownership over the eleven (11) parcels of lands/lots and the lot purchased at Brgy. Tanza in CY 2005 have yet to be transferred to the LGU as of December 31, 2016.

The City Treasurer’s Office is still in the process of identifying and locating documents necessary in reconstructing the corresponding land/lot titles.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that remittances to GSIS should be in accordance with the provisions of the IRR of RA no. 8291 to avoid the imposition of penalties.
observations.managementAction: An amount of P1,675,746.82 in GSIS loan repayments and premium contributions which was withheld over 30 days remained unremitted as of December 31, 2016

observations.recommendation: We recommended that remittances to BIR should be in accordance with existing BIR rules and regulations to avoid the imposition of penalties.
observations.managementAction: As of December 31, 2016, a significant amount of taxes withheld remained unremitted to the BIR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management fast tract the implementation of all the unimplemented programs and projects of the LGU and ensure that future projects to be included in the Annual Investment Plan are implemented as scheduled.
observations.managementAction: The unimplemented or partially implemented projects under the 20% Local Development Fund were already implemented during CY 2016. However, 10 out of 14 projects scheduled to be implemented in CY 2016 were not yet completed or implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management fully implement the LGU’s GAD programs and projects so that the use of public funds would be maximized and the target beneficiaries would enjoy the benefits from the programs and projects.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person failed to submit the Accomplishment Report for CY 2016, thereby preventing the Audit Team from ascertaining whether the programs and activities contained in the GAD Plan were implemented.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that management maximize the use of burley and tobacco excise tax so that the tobacco farmers would be fully benefited from said fund.
observations.managementAction: The City was not able to utilize the congressional share in burley and tobacco excise taxes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly comply with the pertinent rules and regulations on cash advances. Accordingly, no additional cash advance should be granted unless the previous cash advance is fully liquidated. Management should require all the accountable officials and employees to liquidate fully their outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2016.

We recommended that Management ensure that all payments to be made for the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects are supported with the documentary requirements prescribed under Sections 9.1 and 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001. Further, we recommended that the lacking documents be submitted immediately.

We recommended that Management ensure that complete documentary requirements are submitted and fund transfers granted in previous periods are liquidated before the processing of financial assistance to Barangays in strict compliance to Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012 – 001 as amended by COA Circular No. 2016 – 002.

We recommended the following:
Road Concreting along Santol Street and Santol Street Extension
The City Mayor should require the Siffu Rocks Construction to correct/rectify the deficiencies in the road concreting project. Otherwise, the contractor and the other persons determined responsible for the deficiency shall be directed to refund the amount of P970,499.56.
Construction of Main Drainage Structure located at Diversion – Bagay – Solana Road
The City Mayor should instruct the personnel responsible in the computation of the Approved Budget of the Contract to exercise prudence in the determination thereof to ensure that the cost of the projects of the LGU is reasonable.  The personnel/officials involved in the determination of the Approved Budget for the Contract should be required to submit their explanation on the excess amount for further evaluation.
Moreover, the City Mayor should require the concerned officials to effect the following measures to correct the deficiencies/observations noted by the COA Technical Audit Specialists:
Additional backfilling materials should be provided especially at the end of the project
Excess 36” diameter pipe culverts abandoned at the project site should be disposed properly
An area of 6.50 square meters of existing PCCP which was demolished to give way to the drainage project located at Bagay Road should be restored at the expense of the contractor
Supply and Delivery of Brand New Meat Van and Four (4) Units of Dump Trucks

Moreover, he should ensure that the supplier delivered brand new units, otherwise, the persons responsible for the acceptance of the units shall be made liable.
Supply and Delivery of Brand New Various Heavy Equipment and Dump Trucks
The City Mayor should require the immediate repair of the hydraulic hose of the wheel loader to avoid further damage.
Land Improvement – Tuguegarao City Hall Compound
The City Mayor should effect the following measures to correct the deficiencies/observations noted by the COA Technical Audit Specialists:
The steel gates and steel grills should be repainted as the applied epoxy primer paint was not enough to protect it from corrosion
The landscaping should be properly maintained to prevent further loss or damage of government properties

We recommended to Management the following:
A ramp or properly situated hand rail should be provided at the entrance of the ABHS so that clients and patients with physical difficulties need not struggle in entering the ABHS.
