We recommended for management to strictly comply with the pertinent rules and regulations on the inventory and control of property, plant and equipment.  Accordingly, the local chief executive should create or constitute an inventory committee. The inventory committee shall be required to conduct physical inventory of all the PPE of the LGU and, thereafter, prepare an inventory report, i.e. the RPCPPE, a copy of which be submitted to the Office of the Auditor. The inventory report or RCPPE will be reconciled with the PPE records or ledgers maintained at the Accounting Office and any discrepancy/ies should be properly adjusted. More importantly, following the pertinent provisions of the new Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS), necessary reclassifications and the corresponding explanatory notes should be taken up.
observations.managementAction: The responsible offices conducted physical inventory of PPE and livestock, and other agricultural, fishery and forestry products. The physical inventory report would be prepared and any discrepancies between the inventory report and accounting records would be properly adjusted. Obsolete and unserviceable properties were identified and disposed. (See JEV 2016-11-008899 and JEV 2016-12-010388)

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management to strictly comply with the pertinent rules and regulations on cash advances. Accordingly, no additional cash advance should be granted unless the previous cash advance is fully liquidated. Also, management should determine the status or whereabouts of the other accountable officials and employees whose status are neither permanent, casual, job order, or elected. Thereafter, management should require all the accountable officials and employees, including those who have already resigned, transferred to other agency, or retired from the service, to liquidate fully their outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2015.
observations.managementAction:
The practice of allowing additional cash advances to officials and employees who have outstanding cash advances had been discontinued. However, a significant amount of cash advances granted during the current year and carried forward from previous periods remain unliquidated as of December 31, 2016.

After the issuance of demand letters to officials and employees with outstanding or unsettled cash advances as of December 31, 2016, copies of official receipts and liquidation reports evidencing settlement or liquidation of cash advances amounting to P1,260,706.69 was already submitted.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the City Accountant regularly prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements in compliance with P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-11. In doing so, necessary adjustments shall be promptly made to reconcile the bank and ledger balances and so with the cashbook balance of the City Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant regularly prepares the monthly bank reconciliation statements every time bank statements are available and necessary adjustments are promptly made to reconcile the bank and ledger balances and so with the cashbook balance of the City Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the City Accountant exert efforts to reconcile the reciprocal accounts by identifying the causes of discrepancies in the corresponding subsidiary ledgers and to effect promptly the necessary correcting entries in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction:
The City Accountant is in the process of reconciling the reciprocal accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds.

As of December 31, 2016, the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds had a balance of P443,527,086.02 and P444,148,296.76, leaving a discrepancy of P621,209.94.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management comply strictly with the provisions of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 63 and cause the refund of honoraria paid to barangay officials.
observations.managementAction:
The City has already discontinued the grant of additional monthly honorarium of officials and employees of the Barangay.

Concerned Barangay officials and employees have started returning/ refunding the additional honoraria they received from the City Government.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the City Mayor to implement the Decision of the CSC in the administrative case filed against the City employee to comply with the provisions of the Revised Rules on Administrative Cases in the Civil Service and to avoid the further incurrence of irregular expenditures.
observations.managementAction: In his Memorandum Order No. 075 series of 2017 dated March 7, 2017, the City Mayor terminated the services of the employee involved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management to ensure compliance with the prescribed guidelines and documentary requirements for the rendition and payment of overtime services. Accordingly, considering that the subject-overtime services were part of the regular routine work and activities of the employees concerned, they should submit copies of the detailed work program specifying the quantity of work to be done and the quantified overtime accomplishment duly signed by them and their respective supervisors. With respect to the excess payments totaling P11,237.26, the employees concerned should refund the same.
observations.managementAction: The concerned employees have partially refunded/ returned the excess payments of overtime pay.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management to ensure that payments of registration fees for attendance in seminars conducted by non-government organizations or private institutions must be in accordance with the rates prescribed under DBM National Budget Circular No. 486.
observations.managementAction: The concerned employees have already partially returned/ refunded the excess payments of registration fees disallowed in audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management discontinue the practice of allowing the claim of actual hotel expenditures without the certification of the Head of the Agency as to the absolute necessity of the expenses in compliance with Section 4 of Executive Order No. 298 and Section 1.2.4.1 of COA Circular No. 2012 – 001. Further, we recommended that in the absence of said certification, City officials and employees should be allowed to claim only the P400.00 per day prescribed for hotel/lodging.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office see to it that certification of the head of the agency as to the absolute necessity of actual hotel expenditures is attached to liquidation reports and reimbursement vouchers whenever actual expenses for hotel accommodation are being claimed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management charge repair and maintenance expenses of its construction equipment against the appropriate fund source and ensure that only those expenses necessary for the fulfillment of the purposes for which these are received are charged against the trust fund in faithful adherence to Section 84 (2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management discontinued the practice of allowing the charging of inappropriate expenses against funds received from various national government agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management discontinue the practice of charging regular operating expenses of District Offices against the Special Education Fund and ensure that the relevant provisions of Republic Act No. 5447 and the Letter of Instructions No. 1462 are faithfully complied with.
observations.managementAction: The City Government no longer charged regular operating expenses of the DepEd District Offices against the Special Education Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management to ensure that only those expenditures related to the implementation of DRRM activities as stated in Section 5 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 be charged against the 5% Local DRRM Fund and that regular expenses for MOOE and Capital Outlay of the City DRRM Office be charged against the appropriate fund source in accordance with Sections 4.3 and 4.4 of DBM-CSC-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014 – 1.
observations.managementAction: The City Government ensures that only those expenditures related to the implementation of DRRM activities and that regular expenses for MOOE and Capital Outlay of the City DRRM Office be charged against the appropriate fund source.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following:  Purchase of various construction and heavy equipment • The TWG should exercise prudence in the conduct of post-qualification and verify carefully the statements made and documents submitted by the bidder to ensure that their bids are responsive to the prescribed requirements. Purchase of motor vehicles • The Bids and Awards Committee must ensure that they post the Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and approved contract in the PhilGEPS for all their procurement activities. Management should ensure that warranty security in form of retention money or special bank guarantee be submitted to guarantee the performance of the supplier to repair the defects, if any, on the motor vehicles within the warranty period. • The Bids and Awards Committee should keep record and file of all bidding documents and other supporting documents. Also, they should ensure that all amendments to the bidding documents are covered by Supplemental/Bid Bulletins which are duly posted in the PhilGEPS.  Concrete Re-blocking and Purchase of Agricultural Tractor • The Bids and Awards Committee must ensure that they post the Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and approved Contract in the PhilGEPS for all their procurement activities, invite three observers in all stages of the procurement process, and prepare and sign the Results of the Eligibility Screening and the Bid Evaluation Report. Purchase of communication gadget and service vehicle • The Bids and Awards Committee should ensure that advertisements of invitation to bid for the City’s procurement of various projects be valid only until the deadline for the submission and receipt of bids from prospective bidders and do not extend beyond this date.
observations.managementAction: Some  provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 were not faithfully observed in the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects for CY 2016

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the City Social Welfare Development Office to monitor the implementation of Supplemental Feeding Program by the Day Care Workers and the Day Care Service Parent Group (DCSPG) to ensure that the purpose of the program is achieved. Further, the Day Care Worker should collect daily the official receipts/sales invoices and at the same time, update the monthly liquidation report together with the parent-in-charge.  The parent-in-charge of the marketing of food items and preparation of food should also sign in the report to identify the persons directly involved in the utilization of the fund and the preparation of food for accountability purposes. 16.13. Furthermore, with respect to the variance of P16,151.57: • They should determine the person responsible for the tampering of official receipts/sales invoice and require him/her to refund the corresponding amount. • The amount corresponding to the purchases determined for personal use should be returned. • They should submit the official receipts/sales invoices for the other purchases without ORs.
observations.managementAction: Management has implemented necessary measures to prevent the recurrence of the same observations. The amount involved was already refunded/ returned to the City.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the City Solid Waste Management Board to follow-up persistently the approval of the re-submitted Solid Waste Management Plan.
observations.managementAction: The 10-Year Solid Waste Management (SWM) Plan of the City for 2013 – 2022 was already submitted to the National Solid Waste Management Commission and it was conditionally approved. The CENRO is currently working on the lacking requirements for the approval of the said SWM Plan.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that all concerned City Officials immediately undertake appropriate actions to facilitate proper execution of laws and policies governing the revision of appraisal and assessment of real properties for taxation purposes to increase the collection of local taxes.
observations.managementAction:
A new Schedule of Fair Market Values (SFMV) had already been prepared and submitted to the Office of the City Mayor on August 22, 2016. This was endorsed to the Sangguniang Panlungsod for review and perusal on August 23, 2016.The first committee hearing for the approval of the new SFMV was held last September 8, 2016.

However, the new Schedule of Fair Market Values remains unapproved as of December 31, 2016. It was disclosed that the review of the new schedule shall resume after the amnesty of fines and penalties on real property taxes has already been completed on March 31, 2017.
